review_title: A Reliable and Efficient Cross-Browser Testing Platform
description: My overall experience with LambdaTest has been highly positive. The platform provides a comprehensive and user-friendly solution for cross-browser testing, making it incredibly easy to test websites and web applications across a wide range of browsers, operating systems, and devices. The intuitive interface and seamless navigation allowed me to quickly set up and execute tests without any hassle.
author: Navneet S.
pros: LambdaTest's intuitive interface and extensive browser compatibility testing have significantly streamlined our testing processes.
cons: To date, we have not encountered any issues while using LambdaTest.

review_title: This service is a life-saver
description: We are using this service for about a year now and only had to read through documentation once because the UI is so intuitive. Because of this we did not have to reach out to customer support once.
author: Jörn M.
pros: I especially like the ease of being able to test our sites in a multitude of different browsers and operating systems without having to have the actual systems around.
cons: In the absolute beginning of our test journey we tried accessing one of our private sites that was not accessible on the open internet at that time and it took us a few minutes to download and enable the integrated tunnel - but that was entirely on us, the UI did not hide that feature at all.

review_title: Real device cloud that’s rarely available
description: Overall experience has been disappointing. Picked testmu because it was cheaper. During the trial the platform looked promising, but in regular use real device availability has been inconsistent, especially for new iPhones and Ultra devices. We frequently encounter “Device Not Available,” Because we rely on manual testing across specific devices for global clients, predictable access is critical. The gap between listed devices and what can actually be launched reduced confidence in using the platform for scheduled validation or release work. While basic sessions and recording are straightforward to use, reliability of real devices has not met expectations, so it has been difficult to depend on.
author: Eshani N.
pros: It’s easy to start using and requires minimal setup. UI is also clean The session recording feature is useful for capturing issues and sharing exact behavior with clients or developers during manual testing.
cons: Real device availability has been a major issue in our use. New iPhones and Ultra devices are frequently unavailable or show “Device Not Available,” sometimes for long periods. Specially during our peak hours. There is also a gap between the device list you see on Free Trial and what can actually be accessed at the exact same time. Devices appear selectable but cannot be launched on paid plan when needed, making me question the entire point of this Over time, we also saw reliability decline, with sessions failing to start or dropping during manual testing more often than expected.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Excellant FeedBack working on Cloud devices from LambdaTest
description: It has been a good experience working with the lambda test and it provides us a great support in terms of like app testing (UAT OR production apps), so it was a great platform in which we use to upload our apps through Google drive or manually and then it used to test across many cloud platform ranging from Android to iOS and there are good provisions in which you know you can add multiple devices
author: KETAN J.
pros: TestMU AI (prior LambdaTest) is an excellent cloud based testing platform which helped us testing out apps over multiple cloud devices. It provides many variations in which a user can select ranging from Android to iOS, with sim without sin devices. Excellant tool
cons: Basically the sessions recordings were not great and few of the devices were responding slow subject to their data centres reach and availablity

review_title: A Powerful and Smooth Testing Experience
description: My overall experience with TestMu AI has been extremely positive. It streamlined my testing workflow, reduced repetitive tasks through smart automation, and provided very stable results. The platform feels modern, responsive, and thoughtfully designed. It noticeably boosts productivity and makes testing much more pleasant and efficient. I would definitely recommend it to other QA engineers and teams looking for a reliable testing solution.
author: Yury K.
pros: TestMu AI pleasantly surprised me with its accuracy, speed, and ease of use. The interface is intuitive, the features are powerful, and the automation saves a huge amount of time during testing. I especially appreciate how smoothly test management and browser/device testing work. Everything feels polished and reliable.
cons: I honestly didn’t encounter anything significant to complain about. Some features could be expanded even further in the future, but the current experience is already excellent.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: TestMU AI Review
description: TestMU AI has been super helpful for our QA process. Their wide support for multiple devices and browsers lets us quickly check our links across different environments without any hassle. It saves us time, catches issues early, and makes our testing way more reliable.
author: Aastha B.
pros: Excellent support for testing across a wide range of devices and browsers, making cross-platform checks easy. Helps catch compatibility issues early by letting you run the same link on different environments. Saves time compared to maintaining your own device lab. Simple and intuitive interface — tests are easy to set up and run. Reliable cloud-based access means the team can test from anywhere without hardware constraints.
cons: Can feel overwhelming at first due to the number of options available. Test results can sometimes feel delayed under heavy load times (depends on plan/parallel sessions).

review_title: Boosting QA Efficiency with AI-Powered Test Case Generation
description: Overall, my experience with TestMu AI has been positive. It significantly speeds up the test case creation process and helps improve coverage with AI-driven suggestions. While some outputs still need manual refinement, it serves as a strong productivity booster for QA and development workflows. With the right inputs, it can be a valuable aid in reducing repetitive effort and accelerating testing cycles.
author: Narendra K.
pros: Easy to use and intuitive interface Helps generate test cases quickly, saving time Improves test coverage with AI-driven suggestions Reduces manual effort in writing repetitive test scenarios Speeds up the overall QA process Useful for both functional and edge-case testing Enhances productivity for developers and QA teams Good support for automation-ready outputs
cons: AI suggestions sometimes lack context of real business logic Generated test cases may require manual refinement Limited customization in certain scenarios Can occasionally produce generic or repetitive outputs Learning curve for maximizing its full potential Dependency on input quality — better prompts give better results Not always accurate for highly complex workflows May miss domain-specific edge cases
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great tool for emulating different devices
description: It's a great tool. I love that it simplifies the work and allows all the team members to efficiently build cross-environment-compatible applications.
author: Phil S.
pros: It is super easy to test or replicate issues using the devices our team does not own 'physically'. This allowed us to shorten the time-to-resolution for device-specific or browser-specific issues tremendously.
cons: There are not many downsides for the use cases we have - TestMu AI is fast, directly applicable to our work specifics.

review_title: Reliable Cross-Device Testing for Our Web App
description: Excellent tool that has made cross-platform testing simple and effective for our team. Highly recommended!
author: Daniel R.
pros: What I liked most about TestMu AI is how easily and reliably it lets us test our web app across a wide variety of iOS and Android devices – everything just works well.
cons: No major issues; it’s been smooth and stable. But not all OS versions are available for a given device sometimes.

review_title: TestMu AI Review - Ultimate Testing Companion
description: It's a good value for money since you don't need to buy real devices and can access large cloud testing infrastructure, but advanced features can sometimes turn out to be expensive. Customer support gives quick responses and are helpful when issues are straight forward. It;s easy to use as well with the help of documentation and guidance.
author: Maha A.
pros: It provided end to end testing in one platform including performance, API, mobile and database testing. Moreover, it analyzes test results and can identify root causes and suggest optimizations. Fully autonomous testing reduces huge amount of manual efforts as compared with traditional QA.
cons: Sometimes the devices show as available but sessions don't start or they disconnect unexpectedly whilst in the middle of testing.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Use it everyday
description: It's quick to get started and the team love it.
author: Benjamin M.
pros: Ease of use and time to go to market. It's also great at allow our QR to register bugs.
cons: You do need a dev for automated testing, which isn't bad, but just beware.

review_title: Information about lambda test
description: My overall experience with LambdaTest was positive. It’s a powerful cloud-based testing platform that made cross-browser and cross-device testing much more efficient.
author: Dinesh M.
pros: its cloud-based cross-browser testing platform, which made it incredibly easy to test across multiple devices and browsers without the need for physical infrastructure.
cons: "While LambdaTest offers a great range of features, what I liked least was that the performance could sometimes lag
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Excellent Cross-browser & cross-device testing Tool
description: Overall it was an awesome experience. I was looking for a tool where we can conduct our cross browser and multi device testing where Lambdatest not just provided variety of devices but helped us resolving issues that we had in our application and services.
author: Lakshay S.
pros: - Best UI. - Have all possible config such as simulators, emulators and real devices. - have integration with most bug and ticket tools. - They have shared their open github repo helping non coders to work on automation. - It is cheaper alternative than any other similar too. - They also provide learning courses helping us to imrprove our understanding. - They also have best secured instance enabling us to carry out our local testing. - We can log bugs directly to our ticketing tool through integration.
cons: Some times sales teams are too aggressive in sending emails. They should provide free trial option in their newest feature - KaneAI.

review_title: Streamlined Cross-Browser Testing with Automation, CI/CD Integration, and Real-Time Debugging
author: Srujana D.
pros: LambdaTest is a cloud-based platform for cross-browser testing, allowing teams to run automated and manual tests across various browsers and devices. It offers integration with CI/CD tools and enables parallel test execution, which speeds up the testing process. The platform’s intuitive interface, real-time browser testing, and visual testing features make debugging easy. With responsive customer support and flexible pricing, LambdaTest is a reliable solution for ensuring smooth web application performance across all environments.
cons: LambdaTest can be little expensive and may have slower test execution speeds due to its cloud-based nature.

review_title: Makes browser testing quick and convenient
description: It’s been a reliable tool for cross-browser testing. It saves a lot of setup time and makes it easier to catch visual and functional issues early. Overall, a helpful platform for regular web testing work.
author: Krishna M.
pros: It’s easy to test websites across different browsers and devices without setting up anything locally. The interface is simple, and tests run smoothly most of the time.
cons: Sometimes live sessions can lag a bit, especially with heavy websites. Also, the initial loading time for a few browsers can be slow.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: A Powerful Cross-Browser Testing Tool That Delivers
description: Overall, LambdaTest has significantly improved our testing workflow. It's easy to set up, offers a wide range of browser/OS combinations, and provides reliable performance. The support team is responsive and helpful whenever we face issues.
author: Shriram J.
pros: The platform is fast and responsive, with minimal lag during cross-browser testing. The interface is clean, and test execution is seamless.
cons: Occasional delays in loading specific browser versions. Also, advanced debugging tools could be more intuitive for first-time users.

review_title: Great AI assistant for QA testing.
description: Overall, TestMU AI improved our testing workflow by accelerating test case creation and improving consistency in test documentations. Its most beneficial for small QA teams like us.
author: JR G.
pros: TestMu AI is a practical solution that helps reduce the time spent on repetitive testing tasks. It has ability to generate test cases and test documentation. The UI is straightforward and doesn't feel bloated. The learning curve is lighter compared to other platforms.
cons: There are areas where automation and integration could be improved. Like most AI-driven tools, the AI output still requires human review.

review_title: A helpful platform for browser testing with great debugging tools
description: LambdaTest has helped us catch layout and responsiveness issues early before going live, especially on browsers we don’t test locally. It’s made our workflow more efficient, and with a few UI improvements and advanced simulation features, it could become a near-perfect tool for front-end testing teams.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: We’ve been using LambdaTest weekly for over 6 months to test layouts across browsers like Chrome, Safari, Firefox, and older versions of Edge. The platform supports quick cross-browser checks, and the video recordings with console logs have made debugging much faster. Integration with GitHub and our CI/CD pipeline works smoothly.
cons: Live testing sometimes slows down, especially when testing on older browsers or emulated mobile devices.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Value for money with easy Automation integrations
description: Overall, my experience with LambdaTest has been very positive. It provides a reliable and scalable platform for cross-browser testing with excellent support for modern automation frameworks. The performance is generally stable, parallel testing improves productivity, and the debugging tools make troubleshooting efficient. While there is a small learning curve for advanced features, it’s a powerful tool.
author: Chandra M.
pros: I highly recommend LambdaTest for its extensive range of devices and versions. It offers better value for money than competitors, and the subscription is definitely worth the investment. The platform features robust security and great test management, while the automation integration is seamless and easy to execute.
cons: They can make it less latency like other rivals in same space. They can also add more documentation.

review_title: Quick and a fast way to spin up virtual test devices
description: Smooth handling. Simple to spin up a virtual device, load store apps and run manual integration and regression tests.
author: Christian P.
pros: Quick and easy way to spin up virtual test devices! Very efficient way to perform integration tests across various models.
cons: Inability (or lack of an intuitive way) to create templates, had to manually login to my gmail account and manually

review_title: Best tool for App Testing
description: We are using LambdaTest form last 3 years. It is very easy to use. UI is very user-friendly. You can test your app on 1000+ real devices. Every new device is available here.
author: Sachin M.
pros: Multiple real devices at single platform Both Android and iOS Easy to setup and use Customer support is very responsive
cons: I think speed may be an issue. It take some extra time to start and upload app. I think they can fix this issue.

review_title: Cost-effective service to access multiple browsers over multiple OS and devices
description: It's satisfactory and provides best bang for buck
author: Muhd Hanzelah Zameer K.
pros: Ability to access multiple browsers over multiple OS and devices
cons: Lack of real devices for smartphone OSs, emulated smartphone devices are present instead

review_title: A Reliable and Comprehensive Testing Platform
description: Overall, LambdaTest provides a robust and reliable platform for cross-browser testing. Its wide range of browsers, operating systems, and real devices ensures comprehensive test coverage, while features like real-time testing, automation support, and geolocation testing streamline workflows. The intuitive UI, strong integration with CI/CD pipelines, and collaborative tools enhance productivity. While there’s room for improvement in free-tier performance and real device availability, the platform’s strengths, such as detailed reporting and responsive support, make it a valuable tool for QA teams and developers.
author: Paren S.
pros: LambdaTest stands out for its extensive browser and OS coverage, real-time and automated testing capabilities, intuitive UI, geolocation testing, seamless CI/CD integration, and strong collaboration tools. Its reliability, detailed reporting, and responsive support make it a top choice for QA teams.
cons: LambdaTest excels overall, but areas for improvement include enhancing free-tier performance, expanding real device availability, simplifying advanced feature usage for beginners, and optimizing costs for small teams. Minor UI and session stability tweaks could further elevate the user experience.

review_title: Powerful and cost-effective cross-browser testing solution
description: Overall, LambdaTest works well for UI and cross-device testing of React Native applications and is cost-effective for teams. With improved support for native OS-level features like biometrics, social authentication, and push notifications, it would be an excellent end-to-end mobile testing solution.
author: Niteen B.
pros: LambdaTest is a solid platform for testing React Native apps across multiple devices, OS versions, and screen sizes. It’s especially useful for validating UI behavior, layout consistency, and cross-platform issues without maintaining physical devices. Parallel testing and quick setup add strong value for money.
cons: Support for native mobile features is limited. Testing biometric authentication (Face ID / Fingerprint), social login flows, and push notifications is not fully supported or realistic on cloud devices, which can block complete end-to-end validation for React Native apps.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Lambda Test - Makes your Testing easy and Efficiently
description: My overall experience is good because, as a QA in my company, I have to use lambda tests 2-3 times in a week to test the application and use automation parts also to test the application fastly and make a release of the application.
author: Anant B.
pros: 1. Cross-browser testing is awesome 2. Automation makes easier to test the application on various platform 3. Integrate part is also good You can integrate your github, slack, and Azure. 4. A lot of platforms and there versions are used by lambda tests. 5. As a startup company, we don't need to buy every device for testing; the Lambda test did it very easily. 6. Real-time testing helps you test the application parallelly with development.
cons: 1. It takes time to load the application on a mobile device. 2. Sometimes the older version of mobile could not load application properly and if they were loaded, then it was tough to work on it
